channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Saskatchewan Passes Law Allowing Involuntary Addiction Treatment Amid Concerns
item.description: The Saskatchewan government has passed the Compassionate Intervention Act, which will allow individuals with severe addictions to be placed into treatment involuntarily. As early as this fall, people ...

item.title: Naveed Akram hit with 19 additional charges over 2025 Bondi shooting
item.description: Naveed Akram, the man accused of the 2025 Bondi Beach shooting, has been slapped with 19 new charges while being held in Goulburn's supermax prison. These fresh charges include ten counts of shooting ...

item.title: Teacher and partner on trial for abuse and death of adopted infant
item.description: Jamie Varley, 37, a former secondary school teacher and head of year, and his partner, John McGowan-Fazakerley, 32, are facing trial at Preston Crown Court for the abuse and subsequent death of 13-mon...

item.title: Evaton Residents Struggle as Multimillion-Rand Ablution Project Remains Inaccessible
item.description: In Evaton, Gauteng, over 20 families are forced to rely on just two communal toilets they built themselves, despite the government spending over R24 million on 30 new ablution facilities. The provinci...

item.title: Court told suspect in arson attacks linked to Keir Starmer claimed no knowledge of Prime Minister
item.description: A court in London has heard that a Ukrainian man accused of carrying out arson attacks on properties linked to Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer claimed he did not know who the Prime Minister was. Roman...

item.title: Parents Protest Over Blocked Classroom Handover at Kadawa Primary School
item.description: Tension hit Mwea in Kirinyaga county after parents staged protests at Kadawa Primary School over a blocked classroom handover. The incident happened on May 6, 2026, when Kirinyaga Woman Representative...

item.title: Israel conducts airstrike in Beirut amid tense ceasefire
item.description: On Wednesday, Israel carried out an airstrike in Beirut for the first time since the ceasefire with Hezbollah was agreed last month. Israeli authorities stated that the target was a commander from Hez...

item.title: Harris Farm launches Amazon grocery delivery across selected Canberra suburbs
item.description: Harris Farm Markets has officially launched a grocery delivery service in Canberra through a new partnership with Amazon, marking the city as the third Australian location to receive fresh grocery del...

item.title: Concerns grow over Treasurer’s stance on inflation and government spending
item.description: This editorial argues that Australia’s Treasurer, Jim Chalmers, is struggling to maintain credibility amid ongoing economic pressures, particularly around inflation and government spending. The piece ...

item.title: Updates on 2026 Bengal Assembly Election and Mamata Banerjee's Resignation
item.description: The 2026 Bengal Assembly elections have generated significant political attention across India. Reports indicate that Mamata Banerjee, leader of the Trinamool Congress (TMC), may resign following the ...

item.title: Legal Challenge Over Safe Baby Drop-Offs in South Africa
item.description: Organisations like Baby Savers South Africa and Door of Hope are challenging the government in the Gauteng High Court over the use of 'baby savers' — specialised safe boxes where unwanted newborns can...

item.title: Teacher faces charges over alleged abuse and death of adopted baby
item.description: The news article reports that a teacher has been accused of abusing and causing the death of an adopted infant. According to the title, the individual allegedly told the baby's mother, 'I'm going to h...

item.title: Mike Cannon-Brookes tackles Atlassian’s turnaround and AI expansion as solo CEO
item.description: Mike Cannon-Brookes, co-founder and now solo CEO of Atlassian, faces one of the most pivotal moments of his career as he seeks to steer the Sydney-based software giant through a major rebound. Atlassi...

item.title: Satellite data shows widespread ashfall impact across Albay after Mayon eruption
item.description: Ayon sa Philippine Space Agency (PhilSA), umabot sa 8,544 hectares ng lupa sa Albay ang naapektuhan ng ashfall kasunod ng pagputok ng Mayon Volcano noong May 2, 2026. Katumbas ito ng halos kalahati ng...

item.title: TVK chief Vijay meets Governor to present majority support for forming Tamil Nadu government
item.description: On May 6, 2026, C. Joseph Vijay, president of the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), met Tamil Nadu Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar to stake claim for forming the next state government. The Governor...

item.title: Highlights from the Annual ‘Fantazery’ Art Festival in the Carpathians
item.description: At the end of October, the Dragobrat region hosted the annual art festival 'Fantazery,' a celebration of contemporary Ukrainian creativity in a stunning mountain setting. The festival brought together...

item.title: Davido Pauses Music Career to Support Osun Governorship Campaign
item.description: Afrobeats superstar Davido don make am clear say he wan step back from music for now to face politics for Osun State. During one political gathering for Imole House in Osogbo on May 5, 2026, the singe...

item.title: AI Introduces Mollifier Layers to Solve Complex Inverse Partial Differential Equations
item.description: Researchers at the University of Pennsylvania have developed a new AI technique that addresses one of the most challenging problems in mathematics: solving inverse partial differential equations (PDEs...

item.title: Wike defends enforcement of land regulations in Jabi Lake amid political tensions
item.description: Federal Capital Territory Minister, Nyesom Wike, yan for Abuja sey him go continue dey enforce land regulations for Jabi Lake, even as some residents and religious leaders dey oppose development plans...

item.title: Constitutional Court to rule on Parliamentary handling of Phala Phala matter
item.description: Tomorrow, the Constitutional Court is set to deliver its judgment in the high-profile Phala Phala case, which involves President Cyril Ramaphosa and allegations surrounding undeclared foreign currency...
